# zod-v4-mocks

Mock data generator for Zod v4 schemas. Automatically generates type-safe mock data from Zod schema definitions.

## Installation

```bash
npm install zod-v4-mocks
```

## Quick Start

```ts
import { z } from 'zod';
import { initGenerator } from 'zod-v4-mocks';

const schema = z.object({
  id: z.uuid(),
  name: z.string(),
  email: z.email(),
  age: z.number().min(18).max(120).optional(),
  isActive: z.boolean(),
  tags: z.array(z.string()),
  createdAt: z.date(),
});

const generator = initGenerator({ seed: 1 });
const mockData = generator.generate(schema);
```

## API Reference

### initGenerator(config?)

Creates a mock generator instance.

**Config options:**

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| seed | number | 1 | Seed for deterministic generation |
| locale | LocaleType | ['en', 'base'] | Faker.js locale |
| array | { min, max } | { min: 1, max: 3 } | Array length range |
| map | { min, max } | { min: 1, max: 3 } | Map size range |
| set | { min, max } | { min: 1, max: 3 } | Set size range |
| record | { min, max } | { min: 1, max: 3 } | Record entries range |
| optionalProbability | number | 0.5 | Probability of generating optional fields |
| nullableProbability | number | 0.5 | Probability of generating null for nullable fields |
| defaultProbability | number | 0.5 | Probability of using default value |
| lazyDepthLimit | number | 5 | (deprecated) Use recursiveDepthLimit instead |
| recursiveDepthLimit | number | 5 | Max depth for recursive schemas (z.lazy() and getter-based circular references) |
| consistentKey | string | undefined | Meta attribute name for consistent value generation |

### generator.generate(schema)

Generates mock data matching the given Zod schema.

### generator.supply(ZodType, value)

Supplies a fixed value for all instances of a specific Zod type.

```ts
const mock = initGenerator()
  .supply(z.ZodEmail, 'test@example.com')
  .supply(z.ZodString, 'fixed string')
  .generate(schema);
```

### generator.override(customGenerator)

Defines a custom generator function. Return `undefined` to fall back to default generation.

```ts
import { type CustomGeneratorType } from 'zod-v4-mocks';

const customGenerator: CustomGeneratorType = (schema, options) => {
  const { faker } = options;

  // Override by schema type
  if (schema instanceof z.ZodEmail) {
    return faker.internet.email();
  }

  // Return undefined to use default generator
  return undefined;
};

const mock = initGenerator().override(customGenerator).generate(schema);
```

**CustomGeneratorType options:**

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| faker | Faker | Seeded Faker instance |
| config | MockConfig | Configuration object |
| customGenerator | CustomGeneratorType \| undefined | Previously defined custom generator |
| registry | ZodRegistry \| null | Registry for consistent value generation |
| valueStore | Map\<string, unknown[]\> \| undefined | Pre-generated values from register |
| arrayIndexes | number[] | Array of indices tracking nested array positions |
| pinnedHierarchy | Map\<string, number\> | Hierarchy for consistent generation |
| circularRefs | Map\<ZodType, number\> | Circular reference depth tracking |

### generator.register(schemas)

Registers schemas for consistent value generation across related fields.

```ts
const consistentKey = 'entityId';
const UserId = z.uuid().meta({ [consistentKey]: 'UserId' });

const userSchema = z.object({ id: UserId, name: z.string() });
const postSchema = z.object({ authorId: UserId, title: z.string() });

// UserId will have consistent values across userSchema and postSchema
const mock = initGenerator({ consistentKey })
  .register([UserId])
  .generate(z.object({ user: userSchema, post: postSchema }));
```

### generator.updateConfig(newConfig?)

Updates the configuration. Existing `supply` / `override` settings are preserved.

```ts
generator.updateConfig({ seed: 42, array: { min: 5, max: 10 } })
```

### generator.multiGenerate(schemas)

Generates mock data for multiple schemas at once. Takes an object with schema values and returns an object with the same keys containing generated data.

```ts
const schemas = {
  user: z.object({ id: z.uuid(), name: z.string() }),
  post: z.object({ title: z.string(), content: z.string() }),
  tags: z.array(z.string()),
};

const mocks = initGenerator().multiGenerate(schemas);
// mocks.user, mocks.post, mocks.tags are all generated
```

### generator.serialize(data, options?)

Serializes generated data to a string without writing to a file. Returns the same content that `output` would write. Throws if `binary: true` is set with `ext: 'ts'`/`'js'` — that combination requires writing a sibling `.bin`; use `output()` or `serializeBinary()` instead. `binary` is ignored with `ext: 'json'`.

```ts
const generator = initGenerator();
const data = generator.generate(schema);

// Get serialized string (default: TypeScript format)
const content = generator.serialize(data);
// => "export const mockData = {\n  \"id\": \"...\",\n  ...\n};\n"

// Custom export name and header/footer
const content = generator.serialize(data, {
  exportName: 'generatedMockData',
  header: "import type { User } from './types';",
  footer: 'export type MockData = typeof generatedMockData;',
});
```

### generator.serializeBinary(data)

Serializes data to a binary `Buffer` using Node.js's `v8.serialize` (structured clone). Preserves Date, Map, Set, RegExp, BigInt, TypedArray, `undefined`, and circular references with no information loss. Node.js only.

```ts
const buf = generator.serializeBinary(data); // Buffer
```

### generator.deserialize\<T = unknown\>(input)

Restores a value previously produced by `serializeBinary` or `output({ binary: true })`. Accepts a `Buffer`/`Uint8Array` or a `.bin` file path. Pass a generic type parameter to type the result.

```ts
// Type the result via a generic parameter
const restored = generator.deserialize<User>('./mocks/user.bin');

// From a Buffer
const restored = generator.deserialize<User>(generator.serializeBinary(data));
```

### generator.serializePortable(data, options?) / generator.serializePortableAsync(data, options?)

Serializes data to a portable string via seroval. Unlike `serializeBinary` (Node-only v8), the result round-trips across any JS runtime — Node↔browser and browser↔browser. Preserves Date, RegExp, Map, Set, BigInt, TypedArray, `undefined`, NaN/Infinity, circular/shared references, and (via bundled plugins) URL, URLSearchParams, Headers. `File`/`Blob`/`FormData` are async-only — use `serializePortableAsync`; the sync form throws a clear error pointing to it. `Symbol` is supported: registry symbols (`Symbol.for`) keep their identity, and described symbols (`Symbol('x')`, e.g. `z.symbol()`) round-trip by description with identity preserved within one payload; an anonymous symbol's cross-runtime `===` identity and symbols used as object property keys are not preserved. Implementation caveat: symbols are encoded under an internal marker key, so a hand-crafted plain object whose only key is that marker (`$$zod-v4-mocks/symbol$$`) deserializes back as a Symbol; generated mocks never produce such a key. Pass `{ base64: true }` for a text-safe encoding.

```ts
const str = generator.serializePortable(data);
const b64 = generator.serializePortable(data, { base64: true });
const asyncStr = await generator.serializePortableAsync(data); // File/Blob/FormData
```

### generator.deserializePortable\<T = unknown\>(input, options?)

Restores a value produced by `serializePortable`/`serializePortableAsync`, in any JS runtime. Pass `{ base64: true }` if it was encoded that way. The string is evaluated on restore — only deserialize data you produced yourself, never untrusted input.

```ts
const restored = generator.deserializePortable<User>(str);
const restoredFromB64 = generator.deserializePortable<User>(b64, { base64: true });
```

### generator.output(data, options?)

Outputs generated data to a file. Supports JSON, JavaScript, and TypeScript formats. Set `binary: true` with ts/js to additionally write a sibling `.bin` and turn the script into a thin ESM wrapper that `v8.deserialize`s it at import time.

**Options:**

| Option | Type | Default | Description |
|--------|------|---------|-------------|
| path | string | `./__generated__/generated-mock-data.<ext>` | Output file path |
| ext | 'json' \| 'js' \| 'ts' | 'ts' (or inferred from path) | Output format |
| exportName | string | 'mockData' | Custom export variable name (ts/js only) |
| header | string | undefined | String prepended to the generated wrapper/text (ignored for json) |
| footer | string | undefined | String appended to the generated wrapper/text (ignored for json) |
| binary | boolean | false | For ts/js: also write a `<name>.bin` and make the script a v8.deserialize wrapper. Ignored for json. |
| portable | boolean | false | **`outputAsync` only.** For ts/js: inline a cross-runtime `export const <name> = <seroval expr>` (no sibling file, no consumer dep). File/Blob/FormData + circular; rejects Symbol; ignored for json. |

```ts
const generator = initGenerator();
const data = generator.generate(schema);

// Output as TypeScript (default)
generator.output(data);
// Creates: ./__generated__/generated-mock-data.ts
// Content: export const mockData = { ... };

// Output as JSON with custom path
generator.output(data, { path: './mocks/user.json' });

// Output a .ts wrapper + sibling .bin (mockData is typed as `unknown`;
// cast on consumer side or use `deserialize<T>()` for typing)
generator.output(data, { path: './mocks/user.ts', binary: true });
generator.output(data, { path: './mocks/user.js', binary: true });

// Custom export name with header/footer
generator.output(data, {
  path: './mocks/user.ts',
  exportName: 'generatedMockData',
  header: "import type { User } from './types';",
  footer: 'export type MockData = typeof generatedMockData;',
});
```

**Notes:**
- BigInt values are converted to strings in JSON format (with console warning)
- Date objects are serialized as `new Date("...")` in JS/TS format
- Directory is created automatically if it doesn't exist
- `header`, `footer`, `exportName` are ignored for JSON. For ts/js + `binary`, they apply to the generated wrapper file
- `binary: true` perfectly round-trips Date, Map, Set, RegExp, BigInt, TypedArray, `undefined`, and circular references via `v8.serialize`. The generated wrapper targets ESM (`import.meta.dirname`) and is Node.js 20.11+ only
- The sibling `.bin` basename is always derived from the wrapper path; it cannot be customized separately
- `binary` is silently ignored for `ext: 'json'`

### generator.outputAsync(data, options?)

Async counterpart of `output` returning `Promise<string>` (the path). Required for `{ portable: true }`, which reads File/Blob/FormData bytes asynchronously; non-portable modes (json/ts/js/binary) behave like `output` but write with async fs.

With `portable: true` (ts/js) it inlines a self-contained, cross-runtime expression — `export const <name> = <seroval expr>` — reconstructed by a plain `import` in any JS runtime (Node↔browser). Unlike `binary: true` (Node-only v8 + sibling `.bin`), there is no sibling file and no consumer dependency, and File/Blob/FormData round-trip with their contents (plus Date, Map, Set, BigInt, TypedArray, circular/shared refs). `Symbol` is rejected in portable mode (an inline import has no unbox step) — use ext ts/js without `portable`, or serializePortable/deserializePortable. Passing `portable` to the sync `output()` throws.

```ts
const path = await generator.outputAsync(data, { path: './mocks/user.ts', portable: true });
// consume anywhere: import { mockData } from './mocks/user'
```

## Supported Zod Types

All standard Zod v4 types are supported:

- Primitives: string, number, boolean, bigint, date, symbol, undefined, null, void, never, any, unknown, nan
- String validations: email, uuid, url, regex, min, max, length, startsWith, endsWith, includes, ip, cidr, mac, base64, base64url, jwt, emoji, guid, nanoid, ulid, cuid, cuid2, xid, ksuid, e164, hostname, isoDateTime, isoDate, isoTime, isoDuration, templateLiteral
- Number validations: min, max, int, positive, negative, finite, multipleOf, float32, float64
- Objects: object, record, passthrough, strict, catchall (ignored)
- Collections: array, set, map, tuple
- Unions: union, discriminatedUnion, xor, enum, literal
- Modifiers: optional, exactOptional, nullable, nonOptional, default, prefault, readonly, branded, pipe, lazy, catch, success
- Special: codec, json (z.json()), stringbool (z.stringbool()), file, blob
- Effects: transform, preprocess (refine conditions are ignored)
- Recursive: z.lazy() and getter-based circular references (depth controlled by recursiveDepthLimit)

## Unsupported / Partially Supported

**Not supported:**
- z.custom() and z.instanceof()
- z.function()
- z.nativeEnum() (deprecated in v4)
- z.promise() (deprecated in v4)

**Partially supported:**
- z.lazy(): Errors with top-level union, tuple, or intersection
- z.intersection(): Works with same schema types; ZodObject+ZodRecord and ZodArray+ZodTuple work
- z.check(): Only z.overwrite() and z.trim() work; other checks (regex, minLength, etc.) are ignored

## Compatibility

- Requires Zod v4 as a peer dependency (`zod: ^4.0.0`)
- Compatible with Zod v4.0.0 and above

## Determinism

Same seed produces identical output across runs. Use `options.faker` in custom generators instead of `Math.random()` to maintain determinism.

## Common Patterns

### Generate array with specific length

```ts
const mock = initGenerator({ array: { min: 5, max: 5 } }).generate(z.array(z.string()));
```

### Always generate optional fields

```ts
const mock = initGenerator({ optionalProbability: 1 }).generate(schema);
```

### Never generate null for nullable fields

```ts
const mock = initGenerator({ nullableProbability: 0 }).generate(schema);
```

### Japanese locale

```ts
const mock = initGenerator({ locale: 'ja' }).generate(schema);
```

### Multiple custom overrides (priority: first defined wins)

```ts
const mock = initGenerator()
  .supply(z.ZodEmail, 'priority@example.com')  // This wins
  .override((schema) => {
    if (schema instanceof z.ZodEmail) return 'ignored@example.com';
  })
  .generate(schema);
```
